stimulus: In 2022 musicologist Sofia Reinhart warned executives at streaming platforms who confidently predict that traditional concert recordings will soon be supplanted by short-form interactive video formats. Reinhart pointed out that the notion of an "interactive" musical recording is much older than these executives assume, reaching back at least to the early twentieth-century player piano, whose rolls allowed performers and listeners alike to manipulate tempo and dynamics in real time. Moreover, she noted, the newer interactive formats so prized by the streaming companies still depend heavily on conventionally recorded performances as their underlying material.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It explains the basis for the claim being made by the streaming executives mentioned in the text.
It suggests that academics are better suited than executives to evaluate the future of interactive musical formats.
It provides a historical anecdote about the technological difficulties of using early interactive musical formats.
It challenges the stance of the executives mentioned earlier in the text.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The executives' confident prediction depends on treating interactive formats as a brand-new phenomenon that must inevitably eclipse traditional recordings. By citing the player piano as a much older example of interactive recording, the underlined material undercuts that premise and directly challenges their stance.

Choice A is incorrect because the underlined material weakens, rather than supports, the executives' claim. Choice B is incorrect because the passage challenges the executives without arguing about academic versus industry authority. Choice C is incorrect because the underlined portion is offered as counterevidence, not as a tangential anecdote about technological difficulty.
